result.finops_analysis: {"executive_summary":"Current monthly spend is $47,230, with ~45% concentrated in over-provisioned EC2 and an unnecessarily frontier-tier LLM path for routine document summarization. A conservative 25–35% cost reduction is achievable in one quarter through rightsizing, S3 lifecycle policies, and moving summarization to a smaller model with caching. The proposed 90-day multi-region DR + dual-LLM expansion is not yet justified by the available telemetry and should be de-scoped to a phased plan. Primary risks are telemetry gaps (no granular utilization, no tagging discipline) and premature architectural complexity.","current_state":"Three-tier case-management system in a single AWS region (us-east-1): ALB, 42 m5.xlarge EC2 instances, 6 RDS PostgreSQL instances, S3 document store with cross-region replication on all buckets, and a frontier-model LLM used for case-document summarization. FedRAMP Moderate boundary, ~300 case workers, CUI data, fixed FY budget.","cost_drivers":"Compute: EC2 $18,400 (42 instances at 23% avg utilization) — largest driver and most over-provisioned. Storage: S3 $6,200 across 14 TB with no lifecycle policies; RDS $8,900 with 2 of 6 instances appearing idle. Transfer: $5,100 driven by cross-region replication on all buckets, including buckets that may not require it. Licensing: not itemized — evidence needed. Duplication: blanket cross-region S3 replication; potential duplication of logging/metrics between CloudWatch and any downstream SIEM (evidence needed). Operational overhead: CloudWatch $2,400; no tagging discipline raises ongoing FinOps cost. AI/ML/LLM usage: $2,430/mo on a frontier model for summarization with no caching and full-document prompts (~6k input tokens/request).","risks_and_constraints":"Resilience: single-region today; the proposed multi-region DR is directionally correct but the 90-day timeline is aggressive given no tagging or utilization baseline. Supportability: tagging gaps and 7-day CloudWatch retention make incident triage and cost attribution difficult. Interoperability: adding a second LLM provider without an abstraction layer risks prompt drift and output divergence. Compliance: CUI and FedRAMP Moderate boundary constrain which LLM endpoints are eligible; public frontier API usage should be re-validated against the ATO boundary. Security posture: broad cross-region replication expands the data blast radius. Technical debt: no RI/SP coverage report, no token-usage dashboard, and no per-instance utilization beyond 7 days.","optimization_opportunities":"1) Rightsize EC2 fleet — largest single lever; tradeoff is a short engineering investment for recurring savings. 2) Apply S3 lifecycle tiering and scope cross-region replication to only the buckets that require it — low effort, low risk. 3) Decommission idle RDS instances after a 14-day utilization confirmation window. 4) Move LLM summarization to a smaller/open model with retrieval + caching; keep the frontier model as an escalation path only. 5) De-scope the proposed dual-region, dual-LLM change to a sequenced plan: stand up tagging and utilization baselines first, then DR for the highest-mission-value tier, then multi-LLM only if a concrete availability requirement justifies it.","ai_suitability":"Current state is over-sized. Document summarization for case workers — bounded length, repeat document templates, no novel reasoning required — does not need a frontier model. A small or mid-tier model with retrieval over document chunks and a 24-hour cache on identical documents is very likely sufficient. Recommendation: pilot a smaller model behind a thin abstraction, keep the frontier model as a conditional fallback for low-confidence outputs, and add token-usage telemetry before scaling. Dual-LLM redundancy is not justified until a single-provider SLA failure mode is demonstrated.","recommendations":[{"id":"FIN-001","title":"Rightsize EC2 fleet","action":"optimize","category":"rightsizing","description":"42 m5.xlarge instances at 23% avg utilization. Collect 14 days of per-instance CPU/memory/network, then downsize or consolidate. Target utilization band 50–70%.","estimated_savings":"$7,000–$9,500/mo","effort":"medium","priority":1,"sequence":"After FIN-007 (tagging/telemetry baseline).","assumptions":"Utilization pattern is representative; workload is not latency-sensitive at current headroom."},{"id":"FIN-002","title":"Decommission idle RDS instances","action":"replace","category":"idle_resources","description":"2 of 6 RDS instances appear idle. Confirm with 14-day connection/CPU telemetry, snapshot, then terminate.","estimated_savings":"$2,500–$3,000/mo","effort":"low","priority":2,"sequence":"Parallel with FIN-001.","assumptions":"No undocumented consumers; snapshots retained per records schedule."},{"id":"FIN-003","title":"Apply S3 lifecycle and scope replication","action":"optimize","category":"storage_tiering","description":"14 TB with no lifecycle policies and blanket cross-region replication. Tier to IA/Glacier by age and disable replication on buckets that do not carry DR-critical data.","estimated_savings":"$1,800–$2,600/mo","effort":"low","priority":3,"sequence":"Independent.","assumptions":"Records schedule and DR RPO/RTO permit tiering."},{"id":"FIN-004","title":"Right-size LLM summarization path","action":"replace","category":"ai_rightsizing","description":"Replace frontier model with a smaller model plus retrieval over document chunks and a 24-hour result cache keyed on document hash. Keep frontier as escalation for low-confidence outputs. Add token-usage dashboard.","estimated_savings":"$1,500–$2,000/mo","effort":"medium","priority":4,"sequence":"After FIN-007; gated by ATO-boundary re-validation of eligible endpoints.","assumptions":"A compliant smaller/open model is available inside the FedRAMP Moderate boundary."},{"id":"FIN-005","title":"De-scope proposed multi-region + dual-LLM change","action":"defer","category":"architecture","description":"Replace the 90-day dual-region, dual-LLM plan with a sequenced plan: baseline (FIN-007), DR for highest-mission tier only, then revisit dual-LLM against a concrete SLA requirement.","estimated_savings":"cost avoidance — evidence needed","effort":"low","priority":5,"sequence":"Leadership decision before any vendor work begins.","assumptions":"Program can accept a phased DR timeline."},{"id":"FIN-006","title":"Purchase RI / Savings Plans for baseline","action":"optimize","category":"reserved_capacity","description":"After rightsizing stabilizes, commit to 1-year Savings Plans covering the steady-state baseline.","estimated_savings":"evidence needed — requires post-rightsize baseline","effort":"low","priority":6,"sequence":"After FIN-001 completes.","assumptions":"Budget authority permits 1-year commitment."},{"id":"FIN-007","title":"Establish tagging, utilization, and token-usage baselines","action":"keep","category":"operations","description":"Adopt a tagging standard across accounts, extend CloudWatch retention for utilization, and stand up an LLM token-usage dashboard. Prerequisite for every other recommendation being defensible.","estimated_savings":"indirect — enables FIN-001, FIN-004, FIN-006","effort":"low","priority":0,"sequence":"Start immediately.","assumptions":"Platform team has capacity for tagging policy rollout."}],"evidence_needed":["Cost and Usage Report (CUR) with resource tags for the last 90 days.","Per-instance CPU / memory / network utilization samples (≥14 days).","RDS connection and query metrics per instance (≥14 days).","S3 bucket inventory with object-age distribution and replication flag per bucket.","RI / Savings Plan coverage and utilization report.","Current ATO boundary document listing approved LLM endpoints for CUI.","LLM token usage by request type, plus sample prompts and document size distribution.","Program DR requirements (RTO/RPO) by system tier.","Inventory of duplicated logging/metrics destinations (CloudWatch vs. SIEM)."],"anomalies":["EC2 utilization of 23% across 42 instances indicates systemic over-provisioning, not a single-workload anomaly.","Blanket cross-region replication on all S3 buckets is inconsistent with typical DR scoping and warrants review.","Frontier LLM spend of $2,430/mo on routine summarization without caching is an outlier for the workload profile."],"total_spend":"$47,230","top_services":[{"service":"EC2","amount":"$18,400"},{"service":"RDS","amount":"$8,900"},{"service":"S3","amount":"$6,200"},{"service":"Data Transfer","amount":"$5,100"},{"service":"Lambda","amount":"$3,800"},{"service":"LLM API","amount":"$2,430"},{"service":"CloudWatch","amount":"$2,400"}]}
